author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …“With my voice have I cried unto the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It were enough to say, “with voice:” not for nothing perhaps has “my” been added.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many cry unto the Lord, not with their own voice, but with the voice of their body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the “inner man” then, in whom “Christ” hath begun to “dwell by faith,” cry unto the Lord, not with the din of his lips, but with the affection of his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God heareth not, where man heareth: unless thou criest with the voice of lungs and side and tongue, man heareth thee not: thy thought is thy cry to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my voice have I prayed unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he meant by, “I have cried,” he explained when he said, “I have prayed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they too who blaspheme, cry unto the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the former part he set down his crying, in the latter he explained what it was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though it were demanded, With what cry hast thou cried unto the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto the Lord, saith he, I have prayed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My cry is my prayer, not reviling, not murmuring, not blaspheming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will pour out before Him my prayer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “before Him”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In His sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, in His sight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where He seeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where doth He not see?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so do we say, ‘where He seeth,’ as though somewhere He seeth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in this assemblage of bodily substances men too see, animals too see: He seeth where man seeth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thy thoughts no man seeth, but God seeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There then pour out thy prayer, where He alone seeth, who rewardeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord Jesus Christ bade thee pray in secret: but if thou knowest what “thy closet” is, and cleansest it, there thou prayest to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou,” saith He, “when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and shut the door, and pray to thy Father in secret, and He who seeth in secret shall reward thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If men are to reward thee, pour out thy prayer before men: if God is to reward thee, pour out thy prayer before Him; and close the door, lest the tempter enter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the Apostle, because it is in our power to shut the door, the door of our hearts, not of our walls, for in it is our “closet,”—because it is in our power to shut this door, saith, “neither give place to the devil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is to “shut the door”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This door hath as it were two leaves, desire and fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Either thou desireth something earthly, and he enters by this; or thou fearest something earthly, and he enters by that.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Close then the door of fear and desire against the devil, open it to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How dost thou open these folding doors to Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By desiring the kingdom of heaven, by fearing the fire of hell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By desire of this world the devil entereth, by desire of eternal life Christ entereth; by fear of temporal punishment the devil entereth, by fear of everlasting fire Christ entereth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My tribulation I will proclaim in His sight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a repetition, both in the two preceding sentences, and in these which follow: the sentiments are two, but both twice expressed.…For, “in His sight,” is the same as “before Him;” “I will proclaim my tribulation,” is the same as, “I will pour out my prayer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When doest thou this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being set in the midst of persecution, he saith, “while my spirit failed from me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore hath thy spirit failed, O martyr, set in tribulation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I may not claim my strength as mine own, that I may know that Another worketh in me the goodness I have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And men perhaps have heard that my spirit hath failed within me, and have despaired of me, and have said, “we have taken him captive, we have overpowered him;” “and Thou hast known my paths.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They thought me cast down, Thou didst see me standing upright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who persecuted me and had seized me, thought my feet entangled, “but their feet were entangled, and they fell, but we are risen, and stand upright.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mine eyes are ever unto the Lord, for He shall pluck my feet out of the net.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have persevered in walking, for “he that shall persevere unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They thought me overpowered, but I continued walking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where did I walk?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In paths which they saw not, who thought me prisoner, in the paths of Thy righteousness, in the paths of Thy commandments.…For every path is a way, but not every way is a path.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then are those ways called paths, save because they are narrow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Broad is the way of the wicked, narrow the way of the righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is “the way” is also “the ways,” just as “the Church” is also “the Churches,” the “heaven” also the “heavens:” they are spoken of in the plural, they are spoken of also in the singular.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On account of the unity of the Church it is one Church; “My dove is one, she is the only one of her mother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On account of the congregation of brethren in various places there are many Churches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Churches of Judæa which are in Christ rejoiced,” saith Paul, “and they glorified God in me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus he spake of Churches; and of one Church he thus speaketh, “Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the Church of God.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In this way, wherein I was walking, they hid a trap for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This “way wherein I was walking,” is Christ; there have they laid a trap for me, who persecute me in Christ, for Christ’s Name’s sake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There then “have they hid for me a trap.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What in me do they hate, what in me do they persecute?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That I am a Christian.…For the heretics too wish to hide a stumbling-block for us in the Name of Christ, and are themselves deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What they think that they put in the way, they put outside the way, for they themselves are outside the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They cannot set a trap where themselves are not.…The Pagan thinketh to put a stumbling-block in the way, when he saith to me, “Thou worshippest a crucified God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He findeth fault with the Cross of Christ, which he understandeth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He thinketh that he setteth in Christ, what he setteth near the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will not depart from Christ, so shall I not fall from the way into the trap.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him mock at Christ crucified, let me see the Cross of Christ on the foreheads of kings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he laugheth at, therein am I saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nought is prouder than a sick man, who laugheth at his own medicine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he laughed not at it, he would take it, and be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Cross is the sign of humility, but he through excess of pride acknowledgeth not that whereby may be healed the swelling of his soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if I acknowledge, I am walking in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So far am I from blushing at the Cross, that in no secret place do I keep the Cross of Christ, but bear it on my forehead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many sacraments we receive, one in one way another in another: some as ye know we receive with the mouth, some we receive over the whole body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because the forehead is the seat of the blush of shame, He who said, “Whosoever shall be ashamed of Me before men, of him will I be ashamed before My Father which is in heaven,” set, so to speak, that very ignominy which the Pagans mock at, in the seat of our shame.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest a man assail a shameless man and say, “He hath no forehead.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “He hath no forehead”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath no shame.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me not have a bare forehead, let the Cross of my Lord cover it.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I considered upon the right hand, and saw” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He considered upon the right hand, and saw: whoso considereth upon the left hand, is blinded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to consider on the right hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where they will be to whom shall be said, “Come, ye blessed of My Father,” etc.,…He goeth on to say, “and there was none that knew me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou fearest all things, who knoweth what thou regardest, whether thou directest thine eyes to the right hand or to the left?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If, in bearing, thou seekest the praise of men, thou hast regarded the left: if, in bearing, thou seekest the promises of God, thou hast regarded the right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou regarded the right hand, thou shalt see: hast thou regarded the left hand, thou shalt be blinded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even when thou seest on the right hand, there will be none to know thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who comforteth thee save the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Flight hath perished from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He speaketh as though he were hemmed in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the persecutors rejoice over him; he is overpowered, he is taken, he is hemmed in, he is conquered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Flight hath perished” from him who fleeth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who fleeth not, suffereth whatever he can for Christ: that is, he fleeth not in soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in body it is lawful to flee; it is allowed, it is permitted; for the Lord saith, “When they persecute you in one city, flee to another.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then who fleeth not in soul, from him “flight hath perished.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it maketh a difference why he fleeth not; whether because he is hemmed in, because he is caught, or because he is brave.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For both from him that is caught flight hath perished, and from him that is brave flight hath perished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What flight then is to be avoided? what flight shall we allow to perish from us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That whereof the Lord speaketh in the Gospel, “The Good Shepherd layeth down his life for the sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, when he seeth the wolf coming, fleeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he seeth the ravager, why fleeth he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because he careth not for the sheep.”…In two ways a man’s life is sought, either by his persecutors or by his lovers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then “there is none to seek my life,” he said of them; verily they persecute my life, and they seek not my life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if they seek my life, they will find it clinging to Thee: and if they know to seek it, they know also to imitate it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto thee have I cried, O Lord: I have said, Thou art my hope” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When I endured, when I was in tribulation, “I said, Thou art my hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My hope here, therefore I endure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “my portion,” not here, but “in the land of the living.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God giveth a portion in the land of the living; but not something from Himself without Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What will He give to one that loveth Him, save Himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give heed unto my prayer, for much have I been humbled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humbled by persecutors, humbled in confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He humbleth himself out of the sight of man: he is humbled by enemies in their sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore is he lifted up by Him both visibly and invisibly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Invisibly are the martyrs already lifted up; visibly shall they be lifted up, “when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption” in the resurrection of the dead; when this very part of him, against which alone her persecutors could rage, shall be renewed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fear not them that kill the body, but cannot kill the soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what perisheth? what kill they?…Why then art thou anxious about the rest of thy members, when thou shalt not lose even a hair?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me from them that persecute me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whom thinkest thou that he prayeth to be delivered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From men who persecuted him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it so? are merely men our enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have other enemies, invisible, who persecute us in another way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Man persecuteth, that he may slay the body; another persecuteth, that he ensnare the soul.…There are then other enemies of ours too, from whom we ought to pray God to deliver us, lest they lead us astray, either by crushing us with troubles of this world, or alluring us by its enticements.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are these enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see whether they are plainly described by any servant of the Lord, by any soldier, now perfected, who hath engaged with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle saying, “We wrestle not against flesh and blood:” as though he would say, Turn not your hatred against men; think not them your enemies; think not that it is by their hostility you are being bruised; these men whom ye fear are flesh and blood.…“For they are strengthened over me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who said, “they are strengthened over me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ crieth out; it is the voice of the Church; the members of Christ cry out, “Much hath the number of sinners increased.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because iniquity hath abounded, the love of many waxeth cold.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring forth my soul out of prison, that it may confess to Thy Name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This “prison” has been variously understood by former writers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And perhaps it is the prison which is called in the title, “the cave.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the title of this Psalm runneth thus: “Of understanding to David himself, a prayer when he was in the cave.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is the cave, the same is also the prison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two things have we set before us to understand, but when we have understood one, both will be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man’s deserts make a prison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in one dwelling place one man finds a house, another a prison.…To some then it has seemed that the “cave” and “prison” are this world; and this the Church prayeth, that it may be brought out of prison, that is, from this world, from under the sun, where all is vanity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beyond this world then God promiseth that we shall be in some sort of rest; therefore perhaps do we cry concerning this place, “Bring my soul out of prison.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our soul by faith and hope is in Christ; “Your life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But our body is in this prison, in this world.…But some have said, that this prison and cave is this body, so that this is the meaning of, “Bring my soul out of prison.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this interpretation too is somewhat at fault.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what great thing is it to say, “Bring my soul out of prison,” bring my soul out of the body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not the souls of robbers and wicked men go forth from the body, and go into worse punishment than here they have endured?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What great request then is this, “Bring my soul out of prison,” when, sooner or later, it must needs come forth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps the righteous saith, “Let me die now; bring forth my soul from this prison of the body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he be too hasty, he hath not love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He ought indeed to long for and desire, as the Apostle saith, “having a desire to be dissolved and to be with Christ, which is far better.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where is love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it followeth, “but to abide in the flesh is needful for you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let God then lead us forth from the body, when He will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our body too might be said to be a prison, not because that is a prison which God hath made, but because it is under punishment and liable to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are two things to be considered in our body, God’s workmanship, and the punishment it has deserved.…Perhaps then he meant by, “Bring my soul out of prison,” bring my soul out of corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thus we understand it, it is no blasphemy, the meaning is consistent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, brethren, as I think, he meant this; “Bring my soul out of prison,” bring it out of straitness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to one who rejoiceth, even a prison is wide; to one in sorrow, a field is strait.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore prayeth he to be brought out of straitness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though in hope he have enlargement, yet in reality at present he is straitened.…It is not the body that weigheth down the soul, but the corruptible body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not the body then that maketh the prison, but the corruption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring my soul out of prison, that it may give thanks to Thy Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the words which follow seem to come from the Head, our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they are the same as yesterday’s last words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yesterday’s last words, if ye remember, were, “I am alone, until I pass over.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And here what are the last words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The righteous shall sustain me, until thou recompense me.”
